title: San Juan 2-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: San Juan 2-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 2-day trip to San Juan, Puerto Rico: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro) and Castillo San Cristóbal. Prices from $656 per person (estimate).
highlights: Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro)
overview: Spend 2 days discovering San Juan, Puerto Rico — Tropical climate, warm and fairly stable year-round (highs 29-32°C) with a wetter, more humid hurricane season from June to November, peaking August-October. December to April is the dry season with warm, sunny days and lower hurricane risk, making it the most popular and priciest time to visit. This itinerary blends Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro), Castillo San Cristóbal, Old San Juan streets with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
1 night accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro), Castillo San Cristóbal, Old San Juan streets
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in San Juan
itinerary.description: Land at Luis Muñoz Marín International Airport (SJU) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. With limited time on the ground, head straight out to Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro) — 16th-century Spanish fortress guarding the harbor entrance. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.
activities: Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro)

itinerary.title: San Juan Highlights & Departure
itinerary.description: Start early with a visit to Castillo San Cristóbal — Largest Spanish fortification built in the Americas. Grab a last taste of local life before your private transfer to SJU for your onward flight.

travelTips: No passport is needed for US citizens — just a valid government photo ID.
Visit El Morro and San Cristóbal early morning to avoid both cruise-ship crowds and midday heat.
Book a bioluminescent bay tour on a moonless night for the brightest glow — check the lunar calendar.
A day trip to Culebra's Flamenco Beach requires an early ferry — tickets and cars sell out fast.
US dollars are the currency, so there's no exchange needed and US credit cards work everywhere.
With a short stay, book your must-see attractions in advance to avoid losing time to queues.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable clothing for tropical heat
Comfortable shoes for Old San Juan's cobblestone streets
A light rain jacket for brief tropical showers, especially in El Yunque
Reef-safe sunscreen for beach and snorkeling days
thingsToDo: Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro)

thingsToAvoid: Visiting La Perla without a local guide — it's a real, occasionally unpredictable neighborhood despite its fame.
Traveling during peak hurricane season (August-October) without checking forecasts and having insurance.
Driving in Old San Juan — narrow one-way streets and scarce parking make walking or rideshare easier.
Skipping sunscreen reapplication — the Caribbean sun is intense even on overcast days.
Assuming ATMs are everywhere in rural areas — carry some cash outside San Juan and tourist zones.
topRestaurants: Marmalade — Modern Puerto Rican Fine Dining ($$$$)
La Placita de Santurce food stalls — Puerto Rican Street Food ($)
Barrachina — Puerto Rican (birthplace of the piña colada) ($$)
shoppingAreas: Paseo de la Princesa artisan stalls
nightlife: Casino at the San Juan Marriott

festivalCalendar.name: San Sebastián Street Festival (Fiestas de la Calle San Sebastián)

localEtiquette: Greetings matter — a warm "Buenos días/tardes" before starting a conversation is expected, even briefly in English.
Tipping 15-20% at restaurants and bars follows standard US norms.
Puerto Ricans are proud of their distinct identity — refer to the island as Puerto Rico, not simply "part of the US" in conversation.
Salsa and reggaeton are cultural touchstones; dancing invitations at a club are friendly, not forward.
Old San Juan's colonial streets can be uneven cobblestone — practical footwear is appreciated over flip-flops when walking with locals.
localTransportGuide: The AMA (Metrobus) and Tren Urbano light rail cover parts of San Juan, but rideshare and taxis are the most practical way to reach Old San Juan, Condado and outlying attractions. Renting a car is worthwhile for day trips to El Yunque or the east coast ferry terminal in Ceiba/Fajardo for Culebra and Vieques.
bestTimeToVisit: December to April is the dry season with warm, sunny days and lower hurricane risk, making it the most popular and priciest time to visit.
weather: Tropical climate, warm and fairly stable year-round (highs 29-32°C) with a wetter, more humid hurricane season from June to November, peaking August-October.
visaInformation: Puerto Rico is a US territory — US citizens and permanent residents need no passport for domestic-style entry, only a government photo ID. All other nationalities follow standard US visa rules (ESTA for Visa Waiver Program countries; Indian nationals and others need a US B1/B2 visa) as if entering the mainland.
averageFlightTime: ~15.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit San Juan?
faqs.answer: Puerto Rico is a US territory — US citizens and permanent residents need no passport for domestic-style entry, only a government photo ID. All other nationalities follow standard US visa rules (ESTA for Visa Waiver Program countries; Indian nationals and others need a US B1/B2 visa) as if entering the mainland.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit San Juan?
faqs.answer: December to April is the dry season with warm, sunny days and lower hurricane risk, making it the most popular and priciest time to visit. Tropical climate, warm and fairly stable year-round (highs 29-32°C) with a wetter, more humid hurricane season from June to November, peaking August-October.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is San Juan safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: San Juan is safe for tourists who stay alert, particularly around tourist hotspots and transport hubs — avoid displaying valuables and use hotel-recommended transport after dark.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to San Juan?
faqs.answer: The local currency is USD. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Mia Larsen
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to El Yunque National Forest guided hike and Catedral de San Juan Bautista exterior. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Emma Sharma
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Our trip to San Juan was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Condado Beach was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Daniel Ibrahim
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Museo de Arte de Puerto Rico and Bacardí Rum Distillery tour. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Sofia Müller
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. San Juan Water Sports parasailing/jet ski in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Ava Tanaka
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Bacardí Rum Distillery tour alone was worth the trip, but combined with City walls (Las Murallas) walk this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Noah Santos
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Our trip to San Juan was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Paseo de la Princesa was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Elena Costa
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Castillo San Felipe del Morro (El Morro) and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Lucas Tanaka
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Bacardí Rum Distillery tour and Catedral de San Juan Bautista exterior. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: David Nguyen
reviews.title: Unforgettable trip!
reviews.body: San Juan is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Castillo San Cristóbal. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.
